title: Hymenolepis indivisa
intro: Hymenolepis indivisa, also known as False Rue, is a shrub native to tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and fragrant leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Hymenolepis indivisa include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Hymenolepis indivisa is classified within the family Hymenolepididae. This parasitic wasp is further categorized within the order Cyclocephalinae. It belongs to the superfamily Geotrupoidea and the infraorder Elateriformia. The classification of Hymenolepis indivisa is also associated with the phylum Arthropoda, the class Insecta, and the order Coleoptera.
